claim: Break large features into multi-phase plans that span multiple context windows to avoid running out of context
actionable: When planning a feature that might exceed context limits, ask Claude to create distinct phases you can execute sequentially across separate context windows
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering

claim: Use plan mode to explore the codebase and generate clarifying questions before implementation
actionable: Activate plan mode in Claude Code, let it explore files and patterns, answer its clarifying questions, then switch to auto-accept mode for implementation
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering

claim: Add a rule to be extremely concise in all interactions and sacrifice grammar for brevity to get readable plans
actionable: In your user memory or project rules, add: 'In all interactions and commit messages, be extremely concise and sacrifice grammar for the sake of concision'
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering

claim: Include unresolved questions at the end of each plan so you can provide answers before implementation starts
actionable: Configure Claude Code rules to append a concise list of unresolved questions after every plan, then answer them before moving to implementation
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering

claim: Store multi-phase plans in GitHub issues to preserve them when you reset the context window
actionable: Have Claude Code create a GitHub issue with the full plan including checked-off phases, then clear context and fetch the issue to continue in a fresh context window
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering

claim: Use auto-accept mode aggressively during implementation phase after thorough planning to maximize efficiency
actionable: After completing multi-phase planning and answering clarifying questions, switch Claude Code to auto-accept edits to let it implement phases without manual approval
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering

claim: Monitor context window usage throughout implementation to know when to reset and preserve work via GitHub issues
actionable: Regularly check context usage percentage; when approaching limits, save the plan to a GitHub issue, clear context, and resume from the issue
source_title: How I use Claude Code for real engineering
